items.title: Implicit Neural Representations of Individual Behavior
items.abstract: We study policy representation learning from unlabeled multi-policy behavioral data. Each episode is generated by a fixed policy, but policy labels are unavailable. This setting appears in robotics play, demonstrations, games, racing, and other datasets where heterogeneous behaviors are mixed without annotations. We introduce \emph{Behavioral INR}, a self-supervised generative model that adapts implicit neural representations (INRs) from vision to behavior. Instead of mapping coordinates to RGB values, Behavioral INR represents a policy as a state-action function mapping states to subsequent actions. An episode-level latent modulates this function through FiLM layers, yielding a generative prior over policies and allowing policy identity to be inferred without supervision. Because INRs treat each datapoint as samples from an underlying function, the same model naturally accommodates variable episode lengths and different sampling granularities, as in vision INRs with different image resolutions. We also define policy-level out-of-distribution (OOD) shifts along state-distribution and action-distribution axes, which arise when policies overlap in states or actions but are not captured by standard behavioral OOD settings based only on new agents or environments. We evaluate on synthetic Gaussian random field data, MuJoCo demonstrations with controlled OOD splits, and real-world chess, Formula 1 racing, robotics, and Seek-Avoid datasets. Behavioral INR most consistently improves policy identifiability in the hardest continuous state-action settings, especially when longer episodes, more policies, and OOD splits reduce the usefulness of marginal shortcuts; amortized history encoders remain competitive when policy identity can be recovered from symbolic repetition or low-dimensional action statistics. We release code and checkpoints.
items.llm_evidence_en: self-supervised generative model for policy representation learning using implicit neural representations
items.llm_evidence_cn: 自我监督隐式神经表征用于策略表示学习

items.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Behavioral INR, a self-supervised generative model that uses implicit neural representations to learn policy representations from unlabeled multi-policy behavioral data, enabling policy identity inference without supervision.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究了从无标签多策略行为数据中学习策略表征的问题。提出Behavioral INR，一种自我监督生成模型，将隐式神经表征（INR）从视觉领域迁移到行为领域，将策略表示为状态-动作函数，并通过FiLM层用片段级隐变量调控。该模型无需策略标签即可推断策略身份，为机器人演示、游戏等场景的策略学习提供了高效的自监督表示方法。

items.title: Learning Dynamics Reveal a Hierarchy of Weight-Induced Layerwise Gram Metrics
items.abstract: We study feed-forward ReLU networks with fixed readout and quadratic loss. The aim is to rewrite gradient descent not primarily as a dynamics in weight space, but as a collective dynamics closed in terms of fields defined on the training-set space. For a single hidden layer, the weight variables can be eliminated from the activation dynamics, yielding a closed equation for the residuals governed by a collective kernel that factorizes into an input-geometric matrix and a dynamical co-activation matrix. For deeper networks, the residual dynamics retains a clean layer-wise kernel structure. However, from depth three onward, closure requires a hierarchy of weight-induced Gram operators that mediate information transport across layers. Moreover, the conjugate-field dynamics is governed by operators satisfying a backward pullback recursion, of which the weight-induced Gram operators are the first nontrivial instances.
items.llm_evidence_en: Study of neural network learning dynamics via layerwise Gram metrics
items.llm_evidence_cn: 通过层间Gram度量研究神经网络学习动力学
items.llm_evidence: 通过层间Gram度量研究神经网络学习动力学

items.llm_tldr_en: Derives a hierarchical set of Gram operators that mediate information transport in deep ReLU networks, providing insights into representation learning.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究前馈ReLU网络的学习动力学，通过将梯度下降重写为训练集上的集体动力学，从深度三层开始揭示了层间Gram算子层级结构，这些算子传递信息并影响表示学习，为理解深度网络学习机制提供了理论分析。

items.title: Action-Effect Memory Pretraining for Robot Manipulation
items.abstract: We present AEM, an Action-Effect Memory pretraining framework for robot manipulation that learns compact temporal representations from vision-action history. Unlike prior robot representation pretraining methods that mainly focus on single-frame visual encoding, AEM targets the temporal nature of manipulation, where the current observation alone is often insufficient under partial observability. AEM models manipulation as an action-driven interaction process by interleaving visual and action features and applying masked modeling to recover missing content from incomplete histories, thereby learning action-conditioned state evolution. The Mamba-encoded output of the final vision token is used as a compact history representation, serving as the global context for decoding and downstream control. This design preserves a single-vector temporal bottleneck while keeping inference efficient. We evaluate AEM with Diffusion Policy and Flow Policy. AEM consistently improves manipulation performance in both simulation and real-world settings, outperforming baselines across clean scenes, cluttered and random scenes, and non-Markovian tasks. Ablation studies further show that history-aware pretraining surpasses single-frame pretraining and direct frame stacking, while reducing inference latency and computational cost.
items.llm_evidence_en: representation pretraining for robot manipulation, action-effect memory

items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes AEM, an action-effect memory pretraining framework for robot manipulation that learns compact temporal representations from vision-action history via masked modeling.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 现有机器人表示预训练方法多关注单帧视觉编码，忽略了操作任务的时间本质。本文提出动作-效应记忆（AEM）预训练框架，通过交替视觉与动作特征并使用掩码建模恢复不完整历史，学习动作条件下的状态演化。最终紧凑的时间表示可作为全局上下文用于下游任务，在多个操作场景中显著提升性能。
items.llm_tldr: 现有机器人表示预训练方法多关注单帧视觉编码，忽略了操作任务的时间本质。本文提出动作-效应记忆（AEM）预训练框架，通过交替视觉与动作特征并使用掩码建模恢复不完整历史，学习动作条件下的状态演化。最终紧凑的时间表示可作为全局上下文用于下游任务，在多个操作场景中显著提升性能。

items.title: GC-LoRA: Gated Convolutional LoRA for Parameter-Efficient Acoustic Adaptation
items.abstract: Transformer-based Speech Foundation Models excel in most Automatic Speech Recognition tasks but often suffer performance degradation when applied to domains with mismatched acoustic characteristics. While Parameter Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) methods, such as Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), adjust global attention, they lack the local context modeling crucial for capturing domain-specific variations. We propose GC-LoRA, a novel adapter architecture that injects Conformer-style local convolutional processing into pretrained Transformer encoders. By integrating a lightweight adapter to encoder attention output projections, our method efficiently captures local acoustic dependencies without disrupting pretrained global representations. Experiments across diverse datasets (acoustically-degraded, bandlimited, dialectal, child) demonstrate the efficacy of our approach, achieving Word Error Rate (WER) reductions of up to 10.9% compared to baselines while adding minimal trainable parameters.
items.llm_evidence_en: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning method with gated convolutional adapter
items.llm_evidence_cn: 基于门控卷积的低秩适配参数高效微调方法

items.title: S$^2$COPE: Self-Supervised Concept Discovery via Preference Learning
items.abstract: Current representation learning paradigms force a fundamental compromise: self-supervised methods scale to massive datasets but yield opaque features, whereas interpretable models remain bottlenecked by the need for dense human annotation. We introduce Self-Supervised Concept discOvery via Preference lEarning (\model), a label-free framework that resolves this dilemma. Instead of treating Vision-Large-Language Models (VLLMs) as static feature extractors, \model leverages them as active participants in a self-supervised preference optimization loop. By autonomously hypothesizing, validating, and reinforcing candidate visual attributes directly from raw imagery, our framework discovers novel, structured concepts without a single label. Extensive experiments across natural, medical, and physics domains demonstrate that \model successfully extracts domain-specific concepts where standard VLLMs often fail to generate. By amortizing concept discovery directly into the VLLM backbone through our self-supervised preference objective -- rather than relying on static generation and disjoint filtering -- we achieve up to a 24-point absolute improvement in downstream top-1 classification accuracy on unseen data. Our work suggest that interpretability can emerge through a model's autonomous interaction with incidental visual structures, without any human supervision.
items.llm_evidence_en: self-supervised concept discovery via preference learning for representation learning
items.llm_evidence_cn: 通过偏好学习进行自监督概念发现以改进表征学习
items.llm_evidence: 通过偏好学习进行自监督概念发现以改进表征学习
items.canonical_evidence: 通过偏好学习进行自监督概念发现以改进表征学习
items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes S2COPE, a self-supervised framework that uses VLLMs in a preference optimization loop to discover structured visual concepts without labels.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有自监督学习方法产生不透明特征而可解释模型需要大量标注的问题，提出S2COPE框架，将视觉大语言模型作为主动参与者进行自监督偏好优化，自动发现结构化视觉概念。该方法无需任何标注，即可从原始图像中验证和强化候选视觉属性，实验证明在概念发现和下游任务中均取得优异表现。
